children.label: A
children.text: means the staking or risking by any person of something of value upon the outcome of a contest of others, a sporting event, or a game subject to chance, upon an agreement or understanding that the person or another person will receive something of value in the event of a certain outcome;

children.label: B
children.text: includes the purchase of a chance or opportunity to win a lottery or other prize (which opportunity to win is predominantly subject to chance);

children.label: C
children.text: includes any scheme of a type described in ;section 3702 of title 28

children.label: D
children.text: includes any instructions or information pertaining to the establishment or movement of funds by the bettor or customer in, to, or from an account with the business of betting or wagering; and

children.label: i
children.text: any activity governed by the securities laws (as that term is defined in section 3(a)(47) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 for the purchase or sale of securities (as that term is defined in section 3(a)(10) of that Act);1So in original. Probably should be followed by a closing parenthesis.1

children.label: ii
children.text: any transaction conducted on or subject to the rules of a registered entity or exempt board of trade under the Commodity Exchange Act;

children.label: iii
children.text: any over-the-counter derivative instrument;

children.label: I
children.text: is excluded or exempt from regulation under the Commodity Exchange Act; or

children.label: II
children.text: is exempt from State gaming or bucket shop laws under section 12(e) of the Commodity Exchange Act or section 28(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934;

children.label: v
children.text: any contract of indemnity or guarantee;

children.label: vi
children.text: any contract for insurance;

children.label: vii
children.text: any deposit or other transaction with an insured depository institution;

children.label: I
children.text: personal efforts of the participants in playing the game or contest or obtaining access to the Internet; or

children.label: II
children.text: points or credits that the sponsor of the game or contest provides to participants free of charge and that can be used or redeemed only for participation in games or contests offered by the sponsor; or

children.label: I
children.text: All prizes and awards offered to winning participants are established and made known to the participants in advance of the game or contest and their value is not determined by the number of participants or the amount of any fees paid by those participants.

children.label: II
children.text: All winning outcomes reflect the relative knowledge and skill of the participants and are determined predominantly by accumulated statistical results of the performance of individuals (athletes in the case of sports events) in multiple real-world sporting or other events.

children.label: aa
children.text: on the score, point-spread, or any performance or performances of any single real-world team or any combination of such teams; or

children.label: bb
children.text: solely on any single performance of an individual athlete in any single real-world sporting or other event.

children.heading: .—Business of betting or wagering
children.label: 2 .—Business of betting or wagering
children.text: The term “business of betting or wagering” does not include the activities of a financial transaction provider, or any interactive computer service or telecommunications service.

children.heading: .—Designated payment system
children.label: 3 .—Designated payment system
children.text: The term “designated payment system” means any system utilized by a financial transaction provider that the Secretary and the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, in consultation with the Attorney General, jointly determine, by regulation or order, could be utilized in connection with, or to facilitate, any restricted transaction.

children.heading: .—Financial transaction provider
children.label: 4 .—Financial transaction provider
children.text: The term “financial transaction provider” means a creditor, credit card issuer, financial institution, operator of a terminal at which an electronic fund transfer may be initiated, money transmitting business, or international, national, regional, or local payment network utilized to effect a credit transaction, electronic fund transfer, stored value product transaction, or money transmitting service, or a participant in such network, or other participant in a designated payment system.

children.heading: .—Internet
children.label: 5 .—Internet
children.text: The term “Internet” means the international computer network of interoperable packet switched data networks.

children.heading: .—Interactive computer service
children.label: 6 .—Interactive computer service
children.text: The term “interactive computer service” has the meaning given the term in section 230(f) of the Communications Act of 1934 ().47 U.S.C. 230(f)

children.heading: .—Restricted transaction
children.label: 7 .—Restricted transaction
children.text: The term “restricted transaction” means any transaction or transmittal involving any credit, funds, instrument, or proceeds described in any paragraph of section 5363 which the recipient is prohibited from accepting under section 5363.

children.heading: .—Secretary
children.label: 8 .—Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: .—State
children.label: 9 .—State
children.text: The term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, or any commonwealth, territory, or other possession of the United States.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The term “unlawful Internet gambling” means to place, receive, or otherwise knowingly transmit a bet or wager by any means which involves the use, at least in part, of the Internet where such bet or wager is unlawful under any applicable Federal or State law in the State or Tribal lands in which the bet or wager is initiated, received, or otherwise made.

children.label: i
children.text: the bet or wager is initiated and received or otherwise made exclusively within a single State;

children.label: I
children.text: age and location verification requirements reasonably designed to block access to minors and persons located out of such State; and

children.label: II
children.text: appropriate data security standards to prevent unauthorized access by any person whose age and current location has not been verified in accordance with such State’s law or regulations; and

children.label: I
children.text: the Interstate Horseracing Act of 1978 ( et seq.);15 U.S.C. 3001

children.label: II
children.text: chapter 178 of title 28 (commonly known as the “Professional and Amateur Sports Protection Act”);

children.label: III
children.text: the Gambling Devices Transportation Act ( et seq.); or15 U.S.C. 1171

children.label: IV
children.text: the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 2701

children.label: I
children.text: within the Indian lands of a single Indian tribe (as such terms are defined under the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act); or

children.label: II
children.text: between the Indian lands of 2 or more Indian tribes to the extent that intertribal gaming is authorized by the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act;

children.label: I
children.text: the applicable tribal ordinance or resolution approved by the Chairman of the National Indian Gaming Commission; and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to class III gaming, the applicable Tribal-State Compact;

children.label: I
children.text: age and location verification requirements reasonably designed to block access to minors and persons located out of the applicable Tribal lands; and

children.label: II
children.text: appropriate data security standards to prevent unauthorized access by any person whose age and current location has not been verified in accordance with the applicable tribal ordinance or resolution or Tribal-State Compact; and

children.label: I
children.text: the Interstate Horseracing Act of 1978 ( et seq.);15 U.S.C. 3001

children.label: II
children.text: chapter 178 of title 28 (commonly known as the “Professional and Amateur Sports Protection Act”);

children.label: III
children.text: the Gambling Devices Transportation Act ( et seq.); or15 U.S.C. 1171

children.label: IV
children.text: the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 2701

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The term “unlawful Internet gambling” shall not include any activity that is allowed under the Interstate Horseracing Act of 1978 ( et seq.).15 U.S.C. 3001

children.heading: .—Rule of construction regarding preemption
children.label: ii .—Rule of construction regarding preemption
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter may be construed to preempt any State law prohibiting gambling.

children.heading: .—Sense of congress
children.label: iii .—Sense of congress
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that this subchapter shall not change which activities related to horse racing may or may not be allowed under Federal law. This subparagraph is intended to address concerns that this subchapter could have the effect of changing the existing relationship between the Interstate Horseracing Act and other Federal statutes in effect on the date of the enactment of this subchapter. This subchapter is not intended to change that relationship. This subchapter is not intended to resolve any existing disagreements over how to interpret the relationship between the Interstate Horseracing Act and other Federal statutes.

children.heading: .—Intermediate routing
children.label: E .—Intermediate routing
children.text: The intermediate routing of electronic data shall not determine the location or locations in which a bet or wager is initiated, received, or otherwise made.

children.heading: .—Credit; creditor; credit card; and card issuer
children.label: A .—Credit; creditor; credit card; and card issuer
children.text: The terms “credit”, “creditor”, “credit card”, and “card issuer” have the meanings given the terms in section 103 of the Truth in Lending Act ().15 U.S.C. 1602

children.label: i
children.text: has the meaning given the term in section 903 of the Electronic Fund Transfer Act (), except that the term includes transfers that would otherwise be excluded under section 903(6)(E) of that Act; and15 U.S.C. 1693a

children.label: ii
children.text: includes any fund transfer covered by Article 4A of the Uniform Commercial Code, as in effect in any State.

children.heading: .—Financial institution
children.label: C .—Financial institution
children.text: The term “financial institution” has the meaning given the term in section 903 of the Electronic Fund Transfer Act, except that such term does not include a casino, sports book, or other business at or through which bets or wagers may be placed or received.

children.label: i
children.text: has the meaning given the term in section 3(c) of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (); and12 U.S.C. 1813(c)

children.label: ii
children.text: includes an insured credit union (as defined in section 101 of the Federal Credit Union Act).

children.heading: .—Money transmitting business and money transmitting service
children.label: E .—Money transmitting business and money transmitting service
children.text: The terms “money transmitting business” and “money transmitting service” have the meanings given the terms in section 5330(d) (determined without regard to any regulations prescribed by the Secretary thereunder).
